rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-level state.
Place input/output contracts in component type scope only when using visible features and concrete bounds.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type annexes for visible interfaces, implementation annexes for internal bindings.
Only reference ports or data elements that are explicitly visible in the target scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool — never integer or boolean.
Boolean comparisons must match the port's declared AGREE type; avoid numeric conversion of Boolean ports.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes.
Do not generate range comparisons or numeric expressions over opaque or Boolean data ports without explicit numeric typing.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
